questionText: Choose the correct result for this sticker puzzle. A shiny page is filled to 4/6, and a rainbow page is filled to 5/8. One note says, "4/6 is greater because 4 is greater than 5 after making the pieces the same." Another note changes the fractions to 16/24 and 15/24. Pick the best answer.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can compare fractions with different numerators and denominators
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Fractions: Comparing & Ordering Fractions, aligned to Students can compare fractions with different numerators and denominators.
options: [{"text":"The second note is correct because 4/6 = 16/24 and 5/8 = 15/24, so 4/6 > 5/8.","correct":true},{"text":"The first note is correct because 4 is greater than 5 after making the pieces the same.","correct":false},{"text":"The fractions are equal because both are close to one whole.","correct":false},{"text":"5/8 is greater because eighths are smaller pieces than sixths.","correct":false}]
explanation: Nice thinking! Change both fractions to twenty-fourths: 4/6 = 16/24 and 5/8 = 15/24. Since 16/24 is bigger, 4/6 is greater. The other ideas mix up numerators and denominators or use a guess instead of matching-size pieces.
generationMetadata: {"questionNumber":"Q1","questionType":"mcq","subject":"Maths","grade":4,"topic":"Fractions","subtopic":"Comparing & Ordering Fractions","learning_objective":"Students can compare fractions with different numerators and denominators","focus":"compare_methods"}

questionText: Which explanation correctly compares 7/10 and 2/3 and uses valid fraction reasoning throughout?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can compare fractions with different numerators and denominators
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Fractions: Comparing & Ordering Fractions, aligned to Students can compare fractions with different numerators and denominators.
options: [{"text":"7/10 is greater because 7/10 = 21/30 and 2/3 = 20/30. Since 21/30 > 20/30, 7/10 > 2/3.","correct":true},{"text":"2/3 is greater because thirds are bigger pieces than tenths, so any fraction with denominator 3 is greater than any fraction with denominator 10.","correct":false},{"text":"7/10 is greater because 7 is greater than 2, so the larger numerator always means the larger fraction even when denominators are different.","correct":false},{"text":"2/3 is greater because 2/3 is only 1/3 away from 1, while 7/10 is 3/10 away from 1, and 1/3 is bigger than 3/10 so 2/3 must be closer to 1.","correct":false}]
explanation: The correct explanation is that 7/10 = 21/30 and 2/3 = 20/30. With the same denominator, 21/30 is greater than 20/30, so 7/10 > 2/3. The claim about thirds being bigger pieces is not enough, because the number of pieces also matters. The rule 'larger numerator means larger fraction' does not work when denominators are different. The last option misuses distance from 1: since 1/3 is greater than 3/10, 2/3 is actually farther from 1 than 7/10, not closer. So only the first explanation is valid throughout.
